atomicity.message: Staged payload is exactly the setup inspect probe concurrency bead: internal/scaffold Inspect concurrency plus direct tests; unrelated worktree changes are unstaged.

conditions.message: The staged Go scaffold changes are formatted, hash-bound, and mechanically clean for this lint/setup-probe slice.
evidence: Verified current staged payload with git diff --cached --name-status: M internal/scaffold/inspect.go and M internal/scaffold/inspect_test.go only.
git diff --cached --check produced no output; gofmt -l on the two staged files produced no output in a temp staged-index snapshot.

In an isolated temp Git worktree from HEAD with the cached patch applied to its index, verifier prompts regenerated the expected staged hash sha256:1176d0403ce8061efb7e1711e47ef9c1ba85844571f534a2515a4e7c5a1a1db4 and manifest hash sha256:00e2a9854cb8dcbdde3e942841f405f13b196fd8b3ebe884380dd9f8c0fc8df5.

conditions.message: The implementation introduces one small concurrency helper and reuses existing inspect check functions instead of duplicating check semantics.
evidence: BrightOwl verified inspectProbe/runInspectProbes wraps existing gitHooksPathCheck, burpvalveToolCheck/repoLocalBurpvalveFallbackCheck, gitDirtyCheck, and ntmCheck without duplicating their status logic.
BrightOwl verified the tool/fallback dependency remains in one probe, preserving fallback behavior.
Staged-snapshot go test ./... passed with only the staged payload applied.

conditions.message: The concurrent setup probes do not create fake readiness: probe results still come from the same check functions, failures remain independent checks, report ordering is preserved, and the report remains non-mutating.
evidence: BrightOwl verified runInspectProbes only parallelizes preGit and postGit probe functions, then appends results by probe index into the existing report.Checks order.
BrightOwl verified TestInspectRunsIndependentSetupProbesConcurrently asserts statuses and exact check order after concurrent probe execution.
ScarletMarsh independently agreed the payload does not fake concurrency: the blocking runner proves actual overlap rather than just changing call-order expectations.

conditions.message: The concurrent setup probe behavior and stable output order are directly covered by tests.
evidence: ScarletMarsh inspected staged internal/scaffold/inspect_test.go and found TestInspectRunsIndependentSetupProbesConcurrently with a blocking runner that only releases when watched independent probes both start.
The test asserts check-order stability so concurrent probe execution does not reorder the report.
Temp staged-index snapshot focused scaffold tests and focused scaffold/cmd tests passed.

conditions.message: The staged payload meets the setup-probe concurrency contract: the hash matches, only the two scaffold files are staged, concurrency/order/failure behavior is tested, race-targeted scaffold tests passed, and full staged-snapshot tests passed.
evidence: git diff --cached --name-status: staged payload is exactly M internal/scaffold/inspect.go and M internal/scaffold/inspect_test.go.
BrightOwl reproduced staged hash sha256:1176d0403ce8061efb7e1711e47ef9c1ba85844571f534a2515a4e7c5a1a1db4.

Staged-snapshot commands passed: go test ./internal/scaffold ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Test.*Inspect|Test.*SetupProbe|Test.*ConcurrentProbe' -count=1, go test -race ./internal/scaffold -run 'Test.*Inspect|Test.*SetupProbe|Test.*ConcurrentProbe' -count=1, and go test ./....

conditions.message: Evidence is recorded with exact commands and observations, including the live dirty-worktree caveat avoided by using isolated staged-index snapshots.
evidence: Packet 2702 supplied feature id, target staged hash, manifest hash, staged paths, and condition assignments.

ScarletMarsh independently regenerated the staged hash in a temp Git worktree with the cached patch applied to its index; it matched sha256:1176d0403ce8061efb7e1711e47ef9c1ba85844571f534a2515a4e7c5a1a1db4.
ScarletMarsh ran staged-index source inspection, diff check, gofmt check, focused scaffold tests, focused scaffold/cmd tests, and full go test ./... in a temp staged-index snapshot.

conditions.message: The staged slice is scoped to scaffold inspect probe concurrency and its tests; unrelated dirty worktree files are not part of the hash-bound payload.
evidence: BrightOwl verified git diff --cached --stat is limited to internal/scaffold/inspect.go and internal/scaffold/inspect_test.go.
BrightOwl verified no lint runner, wizard, CLI prompt/state-machine, config, docs, or backpressure files are staged.
ScarletMarsh independently agreed staged paths are exactly the two scaffold inspect files.

conditions.message: The governed inspect surface remains read-only: the change parallelizes existing readiness probes but does not add write, repair, install, delete, package-manager, or hook mutation paths.
evidence: BrightOwl verified Inspect still constructs a Report with Mutating false and runs path/stat checks plus read-only runner probes; no write calls were added in the staged diff.
BrightOwl verified runInspectProbes only executes probe.run functions and stores []Check results; write/repair code paths are not called from the new helper.
New goroutines are confined to internal/scaffold/inspect.go and inspect tests.

conditions.message: Report data integrity is preserved: concurrent probes write to a pre-sized result slice by index, appending happens after Wait, and tests assert stable check order plus set-equivalent runner calls where call order is intentionally concurrent.
evidence: runInspectProbes creates results := make([][]Check, len(probes)); each goroutine assigns results[i]; Wait completes; Inspect appends checks by iterating results in order.

TestInspectCompleteFixtureWithReadOnlyCommands now sorts runner.Calls because command call order can vary, while TestInspectRunsIndependentSetupProbesConcurrently asserts report checkIDs remain exactly ordered.
Staged-snapshot go test -race ./internal/scaffold -run 'Test.*Inspect|Test.*SetupProbe|Test.*ConcurrentProbe' -count=1 passed.

conditions.message: The concurrency change does not widen command authority: it runs the same explicit git/ntm readiness probes through the existing Runner/Looker interfaces with existing per-check timeouts and without shell string execution.
evidence: BrightOwl verified gitIgnored, gitHooksPathCheck, gitDirtyCheck, and ntmCheck still call runner.Run with explicit command/arg vectors and context timeouts; no shell or user-supplied command construction was added.
The only new production import is sync, and the helper bounds concurrent probe execution with a semaphore rather than adding new external commands.
ScarletMarsh independently agreed no new command names, services, network calls, auth flows, secret handling, or filesystem mutations were added.

conditions.message: The staged payload changes backend scaffold inspection concurrency and Go tests only: internal/scaffold/inspect.go and internal/scaffold/inspect_test.go. It does not touch browser UI, screenshots, responsive layout, canvas, image assets, terminal rendering, or any visual output surface.
evidence: ScarletMarsh verified staged payload paths with git diff --cached --name-status and regenerated staged prompt hash in an isolated temp Git worktree.
ScarletMarsh inspected staged diff; changes are probe orchestration, fake runner synchronization, and tests for report/check order.

conditions.message: The intended performance improvement is bounded: independent probes can overlap, but concurrent work is capped at four active probes and dependent tool/fallback checks remain grouped to avoid extra work.
evidence: runInspectProbes sets limit to len(probes) capped at 4 and uses a semaphore channel before running each probe.
preGitProbes groups hook-path separately from tool/fallback, and postGitProbes groups dirty separately from optional ntm, so only independent readiness checks are overlapped.
The blockingRunner test waits for both watched independent commands to start before release, proving the intended overlap would fail if probes were sequential.

conditions.message: Agent-facing setup output remains deterministic and inspect-only: JSON/human report order is preserved, optional NTM/fallback probe failures remain reported as checks rather than hidden blockers, and no lint runner or wizard behavior changes are staged.
evidence: TestInspectRunsIndependentSetupProbesConcurrently asserts exact report check order across concurrent probes, including optional ntm after git-dirty.
Report PlannedChanges, Summary, Status, Fatal, PartialSuccess, NextSteps, and readiness facts are still computed only after all checks are appended in stable order.
git diff --cached --name-status shows only internal/scaffold inspect files are staged, so lint runner behavior and wizard prompt/state-machine behavior are not changed by this payload.
